title: frangula rubra subsp. obtusissima
intro: Frangula rubra subsp. obtusissima, also known as the Red Alder Buckthorn, is a subspecies of shrub or small tree in the Rhamnaceae family, native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a laxative and to treat digestive issues due to its anthraquinone glycosides content. The health benefits of Frangula rubra subsp. obtusissima include stimulating bowel movements, relieving constipation, and potentially helping to lower cholesterol levels and improve overall gut health. Frangula rubra subsp. obtusissima can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, although it's recommended to consult a healthcare professional before using it, especially in large quantities or for extended periods.
